title: Berlin Train Announcements - Quick Summary
items: **"Zurückbleiben, bitte!" means "stand back", the doors are closing.** It is the one line you hear on almost every ride. When it plays, do not run for the doors. Wait for the next train; on the core lines it is only a few minutes.

**Berlin has two train voices.** The U-Bahn (BVG) keeps it short, usually just the destination and "Zurückbleiben, bitte". The S-Bahn (DB) tells you more: next stop, which side to get off, and connections.

**A Berlin train is named by its final stop, not a number.** "Zug nach Alexanderplatz" means the train is heading to Alexanderplatz. Match that destination to the direction you want and you are on the right train.

**The S-Bahn calls the exit side: "Ausstieg rechts" (right) or "Ausstieg links" (left).** It means the door that will open at the next stop, in the direction of travel. Shift toward that side before the train halts.

**"Ersatzverkehr" or "Schienenersatzverkehr (SEV)" means a replacement bus.** Part of the line is closed, so a bus takes over. Follow the signs to the marked bus, usually purple, and your normal ticket still covers it.

**"Endstation, bitte alle aussteigen" means end of the line, everybody off.** If it is not the stop you wanted, you have ridden too far or taken the wrong direction. Cross to the other platform and ride back.
